title: helix-cli: Staging Merge Conflict Resolution & Library CLI Verification
description: This tour walks through a conflict resolution run for helix-cli after a staging refresh merge. The sole conflicted file was src/tickets/index.ts (4 ticket-side commits vs 1 staging-side commit), which the auto-merge resolved cleanly with zero conflict markers remaining. No source code changes were required. The tour covers the diagnosis artifacts, the implementation plan, the verification evidence, and the code review confirmation that all 10 ticket subcommands and the library CLI module (6 files in src/library/) remain fully intact and the TypeScript build passes with zero errors.

steps.description: Start here. The staging refresh merged into the BLD-448 feature branch. Git identified src/tickets/index.ts as the sole conflicted file — 4 ticket-side commits (including the new library CLI implementation) versus 1 staging-side commit. Crucially, the auto-merge resolved the conflict cleanly: no conflict markers (<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>) were found anywhere in the source tree. The library module in src/library/ was entirely separate and was never involved in the merge conflict.
steps.title: Overview: What Was the Conflict?

steps.description: The APL-driven diagnosis asked two questions: (Q1) Are there active conflict markers in tickets/index.ts? Answer: No — grep returned zero matches, the file is 150 clean lines with all 10 expected ticket subcommands. (Q2) Is the library module intact after the merge? Answer: Yes — merge-conflicts.json listed ONLY tickets/index.ts; all 6 library source files, the resolver utility, the dispatcher registration in src/index.ts, and SKILL.md were untouched. The evidence matrix confirmed this before any implementation work began.
steps.title: Diagnosis: Two Key Questions Answered

steps.description: Because the auto-merge resolved cleanly, the implementation plan required zero source code edits. The three steps were: (1) grep-verify that no conflict markers remain anywhere in src/; (2) run npm install to ensure node_modules is current; (3) run npm run build (which invokes tsc) as the primary correctness gate. Any import breakage or type error introduced by the merge would surface here. The plan explicitly states that all 6 library command files and their registration in src/index.ts were only on the ticket side and not involved in any conflict.
steps.title: Implementation Plan: Verify, Install, Build

steps.description: The APL for the implementation plan verified three things before touching anything: (Q1) Does tickets/index.ts preserve both sides of the merge? Yes — all 10 imports and all 10 switch-case handlers confirmed. (Q2) Do any library CLI files have conflict markers? No — merge-conflicts.json excluded them and a repo-wide grep returned zero hits. (Q3) Does the TypeScript build pass? Deferred to the implementation step as CHK-02. This structured pre-flight prevented unnecessary changes and scoped the work precisely to build verification.
steps.title: Implementation Plan APL: Three Pre-Flight Questions

steps.description: During implementation the agent ran the verification steps. Evidence: (1) grep for conflict markers in src/tickets/index.ts and across all of src/ returned zero matches; (2) npm install triggered the prepare script which ran tsc — exit code 0, zero errors; (3) grep confirmed all 10 ticket subcommand imports at lines 4-13 of tickets/index.ts, and the library module registered in src/index.ts at lines 14, 54-57, and 98. The APL statement records: 'Conflict resolution complete. Auto-merge resolved cleanly. Zero source changes needed. Build passed.'
steps.title: Implementation: Build Passed, Zero Changes Made

steps.description: The code review audited all affected surface area: src/library/index.ts (routes list, show, comments), src/library/list.ts (tabular output), src/library/show.ts (section rating annotations), src/library/comments.ts (sub-router), src/library/comments-list.ts (optional --section filter), src/library/comments-post.ts (rating aliases and --reply-to), src/index.ts (library registered at lines 14, 54-57, 98), and skill-content/SKILL.md (all 4 library commands documented). Verdict for every file: OK. No issues found, no changes made.
steps.title: Code Review: All 8 Files Verified

steps.description: The final APL statement confirms the end state: all 6 library source files are present and functional; src/index.ts registers the library module at all three required points (import, help text, case handler); SKILL.md documents all 4 library commands (list, show, comments list, comments post) with usage examples, rating values, and --reply-to support. TypeScript typecheck passed with zero errors. The staging merge conflict resolution is complete — the codebase's major code paths for both the ticket dispatcher and the library CLI module are confirmed clean.
steps.title: Summary: All Code Paths Clean
